blocks.content: She settled into the armchair with her book and said good night to whoever was still up. Dean had already gone to bed. Sophie not long after. Cade was at the kitchen table with a glass of water he wasn't drinking.
They sat in the low light. The television was off. The house had settled into the quiet of a place with too much happening inside it for anyone to actually rest.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: Wren told me something tonight. Before she went up.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: That Saturday night — she came back from the bathroom and someone wasn't at the table. Just for a few minutes. She didn't think about it at the time.
Nadia looked at him over her book.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: She doesn't know. Can't be sure. She's going to tell Davenport.

blocks.speaker: Nadia
blocks.content: She should. If she remembers something she should tell him.
Nadia turned a page. He looked at his water. The clock on the wall. The sound of the ocean.

blocks.speaker: Nadia
blocks.content: It could have been any of us. We were all moving around that night.
She read. He sat. The quiet held between them like something fragile neither of them wanted to disturb.
